title: Caffeine Capability Test App - Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Create dark-themed single-page app layout with 11 feature test cards displaying status badges and limitation explanations
acceptanceCriteria: Dark color scheme applied consistently across the app
All 11 feature cards are visible in a responsive grid layout
Each card displays a colored status badge
Limitation explanations are clearly visible where applicable

file_operations.description: Replace the existing App component with a single-page layout that displays 11 feature test cards in a responsive grid. Remove routing logic and create a simple container that renders all test cards in one view.

Create a reusable FeatureCard component that accepts title, status badge (PASS / LOCAL-ONLY / NOT SUPPORTED), explanation text, and children content. Apply consistent dark theme styling with colored status badges (green for PASS, yellow for LOCAL-ONLY, red for NOT SUPPORTED).
Define dark theme CSS variables and base styles for the entire app. Set dark background, light text colors, and ensure consistent spacing and typography throughout the application.

requirements.summary: Implement Local Persistence Test card with todo input, localStorage persistence, and clear storage functionality
acceptanceCriteria: Todo input field accepts and stores text in localStorage
Stored todos persist after page refresh
Clear button removes all todos from localStorage
Card displays PASS status badge

file_operations.description: Create a component with a todo input field that saves entries to localStorage under a specific key. Load todos on mount from localStorage. Implement a clear button that removes the localStorage key. Display the list of saved todos. Use PASS status badge.

requirements.summary: Implement Cross-Device Sync Test card with Increment button calling Motoko backend to update global counter
acceptanceCriteria: Button calls backend increment method
Counter value updates in real-time after increment
Multiple users see the same counter value
Card displays PASS status badge

file_operations.description: Create a component that displays the global counter value fetched from the backend getCounter method. Implement an Increment button that calls incrementAndGetCounter and updates the displayed value. Use React Query for data fetching and cache invalidation. Display PASS status badge.
Create custom hooks useGetCounter and useIncrementCounter using React Query and the backend actor methods getCounter and incrementAndGetCounter. Handle loading states and errors appropriately.

requirements.summary: Implement Leaderboard Test card with form to submit name/score and display global leaderboard from backend
acceptanceCriteria: Form accepts name and numeric score input
Submit button calls backend add method
Leaderboard displays all entries sorted by score
Multiple users see the same leaderboard data
Card displays PASS status badge

file_operations.description: Create a component with a form containing name (text) and score (number) inputs. On submit, call addLeaderboardEntry backend method. Display the leaderboard entries fetched from getLeaderboard, sorted by score descending. Use React Query for data fetching and mutations. Display PASS status badge.

Create custom hooks useGetLeaderboard and useAddLeaderboardEntry using React Query and the backend methods getLeaderboard and addLeaderboardEntry. Handle BigInt conversion for scores and implement proper cache invalidation after mutations.

requirements.summary: Implement External API Fetch Test card that fetches from public REST API with error handling
acceptanceCriteria: Button triggers fetch to a public API endpoint
Successful response displays formatted JSON
Network errors display clear error message
Card shows PASS if successful, NOT SUPPORTED if blocked

file_operations.description: Create a component with a button that fetches data from a public API (e.g., https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts/1). Display the JSON response in a formatted, readable way. Handle errors gracefully and show NOT SUPPORTED status if requests are blocked, otherwise show PASS status. Use try-catch for error handling.

requirements.summary: Implement API Route/Backend Test card that calls Motoko backend test method and displays JSON response
acceptanceCriteria: Button calls backend test method
Response displays formatted JSON
Explanation describes Motoko canister method architecture
Card displays PASS status badge

file_operations.description: Create a component with a button that calls the testJsonResponse backend method. Display the returned JSON in a formatted way. Include an explanation that Motoko canisters provide backend functionality through actor methods rather than traditional REST routes. Display PASS status badge.

requirements.summary: Implement Email Capability Test card with form generating mailto link with pre-populated fields
acceptanceCriteria: Form captures name and message input
Generated mailto link is displayed as text
Link includes all specified fields with proper encoding
Button opens default email client with pre-filled content
Explanation clarifies mailto is a fallback approach
Card displays PASS status badge

file_operations.description: Create a component with a form containing name and message fields. Generate a mailto link with To: brianvbn@gmail.com, Subject: 'Caffeine Test Submission - [Name]', and Body containing name, message, current timestamp, and a random session ID. Display the generated mailto link as text. Implement a 'Send via Email Client' button that opens the mailto link. Include explanation that direct email sending requires external services and mailto is a fallback. Display PASS status badge.

requirements.summary: Integrate Internet Identity authentication with login/logout and protected feature cards
acceptanceCriteria: Login button triggers Internet Identity authentication flow
After login, user's principal ID is displayed
Protected cards are only accessible when authenticated
Logout button clears authentication state
Card displays PASS status badge

file_operations.description: Create a component that displays the current authentication status. Show the user's principal ID when authenticated. Include login/logout buttons using the useInternetIdentity hook from the authorization component. Display PASS status badge. Use the authorization component as documented in the selected components, verifying the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a reusable login/logout button component following the authorization component's usage guidelines. Handle login, logout, and loading states. On logout, clear the React Query cache. Use the authorization component as documented in the selected components, verifying the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a modal component that prompts authenticated users without a profile to enter their name. Call saveCallerUserProfile after name submission. Use the useGetCallerUserProfile hook following the authorization component's pattern to prevent modal flash on load. Use the authorization component as documented in the selected components, verifying the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create custom hooks useGetCallerUserProfile and useSaveCallerUserProfile using React Query and the backend methods getCallerUserProfile and saveCallerUserProfile. Implement the flash prevention pattern from the authorization component documentation. Use the authorization component as documented in the selected components, verifying the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wrap protected feature cards (Cross-Device Sync, Leaderboard, File Upload) with authentication checks. Display the LoginButton component prominently. Show the ProfileSetup modal when an authenticated user has no profile. Ensure the app requires login before showing application data, following the authorization component's guidelines. Use the authorization component as documented in the selected components, verifying the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Implement File Upload Test card with image upload, preview, backend persistence, and global gallery
acceptanceCriteria: File input accepts image uploads
Selected image displays local preview
Upload button sends image to backend
Gallery displays all globally uploaded images
Multiple users see the same uploaded images
Card displays PASS status badge

file_operations.description: Create a component with a file input that accepts images. Display a local preview of the selected image. Implement an upload button that calls uploadImage backend method using ExternalBlob.fromBytes with upload progress tracking. Fetch and display all uploaded images from getAllImages backend method in a gallery grid. Each image should use ExternalBlob.getDirectURL() for display. Require authentication for upload functionality. Display PASS status badge. Use the blob-storage component as documented in the selected components, verifying the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create custom hooks useUploadImage and useGetAllImages using React Query, the backend methods uploadImage and getAllImages, and the ExternalBlob class. Handle file-to-bytes conversion, upload progress tracking using withUploadProgress, and proper cache invalidation. Use the blob-storage component as documented in the selected components, verifying the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Implement Real-Time Test card explaining WebSocket limitations with NOT SUPPORTED status
acceptanceCriteria: Card displays NOT SUPPORTED status badge
Explanation clearly states WebSocket limitations
Alternative polling pattern is mentioned
No simulated real-time functionality is implemented

file_operations.description: Create a component that displays NOT SUPPORTED status badge. Include a clear explanation that WebSockets and Socket.io are not supported on the Internet Computer platform. Mention that polling or query-based updates are alternative patterns for near-real-time functionality. Do not implement any simulated real-time features.

requirements.summary: Implement Version/Deploy Test card with version number, editable changelog, and deployment info
acceptanceCriteria: Version number 'v1.0' is prominently displayed
Changelog textarea allows editing
Changelog content persists in localStorage
Deployment environment info is shown
Card displays PASS status badge

file_operations.description: Create a component displaying version number 'v1.0' prominently. Include an editable textarea for changelog that persists to localStorage. Display deployment environment information (e.g., development, production, canister IDs from environment variables). Display PASS status badge.

requirements.summary: Implement URL State/Query Parameter Test card that reads query parameters and unlocks hidden section
acceptanceCriteria: Query parameters are parsed from URL on component mount
All query parameters are displayed in a list
Hidden section becomes visible when ?code=237 is present
Explanation confirms URL state works in React Router
Card displays PASS status badge

file_operations.description: Create a component that parses query parameters from the URL on mount using URLSearchParams or React Router's useSearchParams. Display all detected query parameters in a list. Show a hidden section when the query parameter 'code' equals '237'. Include an explanation that URL state works reliably in React applications. Display PASS status badge.

requirements.summary: Apply creative visual theme with distinct colors (avoiding blue/purple), typography, and cohesive dark UI
acceptanceCriteria: Consistent color palette applied across all cards
Typography hierarchy is clear and readable
Card layouts follow a unified design system
Dark theme is implemented without relying on blue/purple

file_operations.description: Define a custom color palette that avoids blue and purple. Consider colors like emerald green, amber/orange, cyan, and warm grays. Configure typography settings for clear hierarchy. Extend theme with custom spacing and border radius values for consistent card styling.

Apply base dark theme styles using the custom color palette. Define consistent typography scales (headings, body text, labels). Set up spacing utilities and ensure all text remains readable against dark backgrounds.

Apply the unified design system to the FeatureCard component. Use consistent padding, border radius, and shadow styles. Ensure status badges use the custom color palette (e.g., emerald for PASS, amber for LOCAL-ONLY, red-orange for NOT SUPPORTED).
